data.title: Technical View: Nifty should breach 24,700 for upward journey to sustain, CRR cut hopes lift Bank Nifty above 53,000
data.description:
Nifty Trend

The Nifty 50 saw consolidation after a strong rally over the past three sessions. Traders may be cautious as the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) began discussions on the interest rate decision on December 4, with the meeting concluding on December 6. The index hit the November high intraday and sustained above all key moving averages, as well as above the downward sloping trendline, which is a positive sign. It needs to decisively surpass 24,700 (the middle of the Bollinger Bands on a weekly basis) for a smart upward journey. The immediate resistance is placed at 24,550, while the 24,350-24,300 range is likely to act as a support zone, experts said.

The Nifty 50 remained above the 24,400 level for most of the session and closed 10 points higher at 24,467, continuing its uptrend for the fourth consecutive session. The index formed a Doji-like candlestick pattern on the daily charts, indicating indecision between buyers and sellers.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Shrikant Chouhan, Head of Equity Research at Kotak Securities, believes that the short-term market trend is still positive, and a strategy of buying on dips and selling on rallies would be ideal for day traders.

In terms of support and resistance levels, according to him, 24,350-24,250 will act as key support areas, while 24,550-24,625 could serve as crucial resistance zones for the bulls. However, a breach below 24,250 would make the uptrend vulnerable, he said.

According to weekly options data, the resistance for the Nifty 50 is placed at 24,500, followed by 24,800, with 24,000 being crucial support.

On the Call side, the maximum open interest was seen at the 25,000 strike, followed by the 24,800 and 24,500 strikes, with maximum writing at the 24,750 strike, and then the 24,550 and 24,600 strikes. On the Put side, the 24,000 strike holds the maximum open interest, followed by the 23,800 and 23,900 strikes, with maximum writing at the 23,800 strike, and then the 23,900 and 24,500 strikes.

Bank Nifty

The Bank Nifty also sustained its northward trend for four consecutive days, with a pickup in actual momentum seen since the previous session, when it breached the two-month consolidation zone. The index surpassed the 53,000 mark on a closing basis for the first time since September 27 and rallied 1.08 percent, or 571 points, to close at 53,267, forming a long bullish candlestick pattern on the daily charts amid hopes of cut in cash reserve ratio by the MPC in policy meeting.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

It reached the upper end of the Bollinger Bands, with strong positive momentum indicated by the RSI (Relative Strength Index at 63.91) and MACD (Moving Average Convergence Divergence).

Now, the banking index needs to hold above the 53,000 zone for an up move toward 53,750 and then 54,000. On the downside, support has shifted higher to the 53,000 and 52,750 levels, said Chandan Taparia, Senior Vice President and Analyst-Derivatives at Motilal Oswal Financial Services.

data.title: 70% chance that RBI will cut rates by 25 bps in upcoming MPC; says Deepak Agrawal of Kotak Mahindra MF
data.description:
representative image

There is a 60-70 percent chance for the central bank to cut the repo rate by 25 basis points along with a 50 basis point cut in cash reserve ratio (CRR) in the upcoming monetary policy committee (MPC) meeting said Deepal Agrawal, CIO of fixed income at Kotak Mahindra Mutual Fund.

"If the RBI revises its growth projection downward by 70 basis points and raises its inflation projection by 10 to 20 basis points for FY25, there is a strong case for a rate cut," he said at the launch of Kotak AMC's market outlook for 2025.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The weak GDP growth in Q2FY24, at Rs 5.4 lakh crore, compared to Rs 6.7 lakh crore in Q1FY24, results in an average first-half growth of around 6 percent, he said. "Based on GST collections, H2FY24 growth may also remain weak, likely in the range of 6.5 percent to 7 percent, rather than the anticipated 7.5 percent. This will take the full-year GDP growth 70 basis points lower than RBI's projection of 7.2 percent," he said.

The MPC of the central bank has begun its three-day meeting on Wednesday to decide on interest rates and its policy stance. In its last policy meeting in October, the MPC kept the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5 percent for the tenth straight meeting. However, the MPC changed the policy stance to ‘neutral’ from ‘withdrawal of accommodation’.

In the past month, the currency has depreciated from 84.2 to 84.7, and the RBI has been using its reserves to defend it, said Agrawal. Despite this, and with current inflation at 6 percent, the RBI focuses on future inflation when setting monetary policy and by FY26, inflation is expected to be around 4 percent, he said.

Therefore, he says, from now until December 2025, the RBI is likely to cut rates by 50-75 basis points, focusing on the long-term inflation outlook rather than current inflation.

data.title: Lancer Container ties up with Dubai-based OVSL to target 'multi-fold' growth
data.description:
The shipping industry is seeing significant growth, with the government aiming to double the share of waterways in the overall transport mix from 6% to 12% by 2025.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shipping and logistics services company Lancer Containers Lines has tied up with Dubai-based Ocean Voyage Shipping Line LLC (OVSL) in an effort to expand globally.

The shipping and container player said in an exchange filing on December 4 that it is entering in a 'strategic collaboration' for 'chartering of vessels and allied operations' with the Dubai-based company. Lancer expects the tie up to drive growth over the next few years. "With this alliance, Lancer Container Lines Limited expects multi-fold growth for next 3 years with margin expansion as scale of operations continue to improve," the statement added.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

With a market capitalisation of Rs 874 crore, shares of Lancer Containers Lines have underperformed this year with a 57% fall on a YTD basis, having seen its life high of Rs 110 in February. Shares of Lancer ended higher by 3% on December 4.

Lancer said the tie up will play an 'integral part' in the company's development, adding that the company is looking at the expansion of its business operations 'globally'. Lancer added that it believes the pact will result in an enhanced shareholder value.

"...further developments in line with the aforesaid collaboration including execution of the “Charter Party Agreement” shall be intimated to the BSE and stakeholders in due course of time," Lancer Containers Lines informed exchanges.

Lancer Containers clocked a 24% on year fall in revenue from operations at Rs 152 crore in Q1FY25, with a 8.6% YoY growth in net profit ar Rs 16 crore.

The shipping company has been positioning itself in Dubai in an effort to seek advantages for the shipping and logistics operations. "The strategic location allows us to tap into key global
shipping routes and facilitates seamless connectivity with major markets across Asia, Europe, and Africa. This advantage has been instrumental in enhancing our service offerings, reducing transit times, and improving overall customer satisfaction," company's CMD A Khalik Chataiwala had said in its June investor presentation.

The shipping industry is seeing significant growth, with the government aiming to double the share of waterways in the overall transport mix from 6% to 12% by 2025. Initiatives to boost the shipping logistics in India have resulted in greater investment in port and waterways development, along with coastal shipping.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Centre has also put in place to promote Indian shipping companies, including a 12% subsidy on Indian-built and flagged vessels, along with subsidy on shipbuilding and ship repair.

Recently, there has been strong indications that important shipping-related bills could be tabled before the House and cleared.

The Merchant Shipping Bill, a new draft law planned by the government, seeks to ensure compliance with India's obligation under maritime treaties to which New Delhi is a party.

The Coastal Shipping Bill, 2024 will remove the requirement for Indian-flagged vessels to acquire a general trading licence to operate on coastal waters once it gets Parliament nod.

The proposed Indian Ports Bill, 2023, to replace the Indian Ports Act, 1908, seeks to empower the Maritime States Development Council (MSDC) and promote integrated planning between states and Centre with respect to port development.

Centre has also set in motion a plan to foray into the global container shipping business, which will be led by Container Corporation, and has commissioned a study by consultants for a roadmap in this regard, CNN-News18 reported on November 19, citing sources.

data.title: Brickwork Ratings pays Rs 1.54 cr to settle allegations of violations of Credit Rating Agencies Regulations
data.description:
The regulator had conducted an inspection of the credit rating agency.

Brickwork Ratings pays Rs 1.54 crore to settle allegations of violations of Credit Rating Agencies Regulations.

The settlement order was issued by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) on December 4.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The rating agency was inspected to check if it is compliant with SEBI norms. Based on the findings from the inspection, an enquiry proceeding was initiated by the regulator and a Designated Authority was appointed to enquire into certain allegations.

(The copy is being updated.)

data.title: Dhanlaxmi Crop Science SME IPO to hit Dalal Street on Dec 9, shares to list on Dec 16
data.description:
Dhanlaxmi Crop Science IPO

Dhanlaxmi Crop Science is ready to launch its initial share sale for fund raising on December 9, with a price band of Rs 52-55 per share. This would be fifth IPO from the small and medium enterprise (SME) segment in December after Nisus Finance Services Co, Emerald Tyre Manufacturers, Jungle Camps India, and Purple United Sales.

The company intends to raise Rs 23.8 crore via initial public offering of 43.28 lakh shares at the upper price band. This book-built issue comprises of entirely fresh issue with no offer-for-sale component.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The public issue will close on December 11, while the anchor book, if any, will be opened for a day on December 6. The company has reserved half of its net issue size (excluding market maker portion) for qualified institutional buyers, 15 percent for non-institutional investors, and the remainder 35 percent for retail investors.

Dhanlaxmi Crop Science produces, processes and sells seeds for various field crops and vegetables, with cotton seeds contributing the majority of revenue at 76 percent in FY24. As of September 2024, it produced seeds for 24 different field crops and vegetables with operations in 5 states, while it has 1,185 authorised dealers or distributors in 4 states (Gujarat, Rajasthan, Haryana and Maharashtra).

Click Here To Read All IPO News

It had access to farmland in 4 states in India, primarily through contractual arrangements with over 45 seed growers/organizers.

The Gujarat-based seeds producer will utilise IPO proceeds (excluding offer expenses) mainly for its working capital requirements and the remainder for general corporate purposes.

It has reported net profit at Rs 4.65 crore for year ended March 2024, increasing from Rs 3 crore in the previous year. Revenue from operations soared to Rs 63.7 crore, up from Rs 46.6 crore a year ago.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Also read: Purple United Sales set to launch its Rs 33-crore IPO on December 11

Investors can start trading in Dhanlaxmi Crop Science shares on the NSE Emerge effective December 16. Finshore Management Services is appointed as the book running lead manager to the issue.

data.title: L&T Tech Services wins $50-million deal from a global network provider
data.description:
L&T Technology Services

L&T Technology Services Ltd has won a $50-million deal from a leading global network solutions firm to provide product integration services in the North American market to ensure seamless deployment and support for customers across the region, the company said on December 4.

The multi-year agreement will enable the company to provide comprehensive support for the client’s advanced networking solutions, ensuring seamless integration and success for customers across the region, the company said in a release without divulging the name of the firm.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

L&T Tech Services will leverage its capabilities in artificial intelligence (AI) and automation frameworks for project deployment and execution.

The partnership combines LTTS’ deep expertise in engineering and R&D services with the client’s cutting-edge wireless networking solutions, it said.

"By integrating a GLOCAL (combining the word global and local) services model, cutting-edge automation, and AI-driven frameworks, we will deliver value and reliability to our customer. This will help us elevate our partnership with the client further,” L&T Technology Services CEO & managing director Amit Chadha was quoted as saying in the release.

The companies also aim to secure LTE and 5G private networks across industries, unlocking new opportunities and enabling future-ready operations.

The partnership will address current market demands while positioning itself for upcoming 5G advancements.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: India could be an oasis amid global uncertainty though some challenges persist, says Kotak AMC’s Nilesh Shah
data.description:
Second-quarter GDP came in below expectations, with subdued growth in key indicators like cement volumes, passenger vehicle sales, and corporate income tax collections,” he added, attributing the downturn to weather, lower government capital expenditure, and seasonal disruptions, amongst others.

While global uncertainties persist, Kotak AMC's Managing Director Nilesh Shah believes India could be uniquely positioned to benefit from changing dynamics. “In many ways, the Indian economy looks like an oasis in the global desert,” said Shah, adding that there were some challenges due to the second-quarter GDP numbers.

The country's economic performance in the first half of the fiscal year has been mixed, Shah noted. “Second-quarter GDP came in below expectations, with subdued growth in key indicators like cement volumes, passenger vehicle sales, and corporate income tax collections,” he added, attributing the downturn to weather, lower government capital expenditure, and seasonal disruptions, amongst others.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Shah sees opportunities for recovery in the second half. “The festival and wedding seasons are driving a resurgence in consumption. With around 4.8 million weddings expected, generating spending of about Rs 6 lakh crore, the second half (of FY25) looks better,” Shah said.

Government and Rural Sector Driving Growth

Government capital expenditure is anticipated to pick up significantly in the coming months. “To meet the budgeted targets, monthly government capex needs to increase by 68 percent,” Shah suggested.

Kharif production, he noted, has been bumper, and high water reserve levels indicate a promising Rabi crop. "Rural consumption growth driven by various factors like strong tractor and two-wheeler sales, is outpacing urban demand,” Shah added.

India’s Opportunity in Global Trade Shifts

The ongoing US-China trade tensions provide a significant opportunity for India to expand its manufacturing sector. “India’s manufacturing, which contributes less than 20 percent to GDP, could gain from high tariffs on Chinese exports,” Shah said. Answering a query on what gave him the confidence for India to benefit in the space, Shah noted that entrepreneurial spirit in the country could play an important role, but added that there is a need to encourage entrepreneurs and also provide ease of doing business.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Global Comparisons and Risks

Compared to the US, Shah noted that emerging markets, including India, appear far more attractive from a valuation perspective. However, risks remain. “Will global investors reallocate from expensive US markets to emerging ones like India? That remains to be seen,” Shah said.

Some challenges persist

While India has growth potential, challenges persist. “GST collections are trailing nominal GDP growth, signaling underlying concerns,” Shah said, adding that private capital expenditure also remains stagnant despite deleveraged corporate balance sheets.

With increased government spending, robust rural demand, and the festival-driven consumption uptick, the second half of the fiscal year could see growth, Shah said.

data.title: Axis Securities pays Rs 14.62 lakh to settle allegations of violation of Stock Brokers Regulations
data.description:
The noticed dated June 4, 2024, was sent to the broker, who then filed an application dated July 23, 2024, to settle the proceedings.

Axis Securities has paid Rs 14.62 lakh to settle allegations of violations of Stock Brokers Regulations.

In a settlement order issued on December 4, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) said that a showcause notice had been issued to Axis Securities after investigations prima facie showed that a dealer of the broker had colluded with a friend to front-run trades of a big client.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Also read: SEBI proposes that stock exchanges do online monitoring of system audit of stock brokers

As the order issued by SEBI's adjudicating officer G Ramar stated, "Investigation prima facie found that Vikrant Bhimrao Kadam (worked as a dealer with the Applicant [Axis Securities]) and his childhood friend Mandar Ulhas Bhatkar were front-running the trades of the Big Client in the equity and derivatives segments of NSE. Further, it was also observed and alleged that the Applicant had failed to exercise due diligence as per its dealer and Branch manager compliance policy, which was in violation of SEBI (Stock Brokers) Regulations, 1992 (“Broker Regulations”)."

The noticed dated June 4, 2024, was sent to the broker, who then filed an application dated July 23, 2024, to settle the proceedings "without admitting or denying the findings of fact and conclusions of law".

Showcause notice

In the notice sent to the broker, the following allegations were made:

1.Mandar Ulhas Bhatkar received funds from Vikrant Bhimrao Kadam, which was transferred to his broker Zerodha in order to enable Vikrant Bhimrao Kadam to trade in the scrips/contracts in which the Big Client traded. Further, he had given his consent to Vikrant Bhimrao Kadam to trade in the securities market through his trading account. Therefore, Mandar Ulhas Bhatkar and Vikrant Bhimrao Kadam together in collusion took advantage of the orders/trades of Big Client by front running the orders/trades of Big Client and earned huge profits.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

2.Accordingly, the investigation revealed that the Dealer & Branch Manager Compliance Policy was breached by certain staff members in different cities of India where branches of Applicant (Axis Securities) are located. This casual approach of Applicant resulted in its dealer i.e., Vikrant Bhimrao Kadam in colluding with his friend i.e., Mandar Ulhas Bhatkar, to carry out front running activities using mobile phone from the premises of Applicant.

3.Therefore, it was alleged that as a stock broker the Applicant failed to exercise appropriate due diligence as required in its Dealer & Branch Manager Compliance Policy, and accordingly it violated the provisions of Clause A(2) and (5) of Schedule II read with Regulation 9 of SEBI (Stock Brokers) Regulations 1992.

Therefore, the broker was asked why appropriate directions under provisions of the SEBI Act should not be issued against it.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Infosys partners with Swiss logistics firm Kardex to 'transform its operations'
data.description:
Infosys

Information technology firm Infosys on December 4 announced collaboration with Switzerland-based intralogistics solutions firm Kardex to transform its business operations using SAP S/4HANA.

Infosys will leverage its cloud solution Cobalt to unfiy Kardex's enterprise pesource planning (ERP) system in over 30 countries.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Leveraging cloud solution Infosys Cobalt, Kardex's Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system in over 30 countries, operational efficiency, and scalability will be unified.

"Infosys will help Kardex harmonise and standardise its business processes to drive productivity, flexibility, and improved decision-making," the Bengaluru-headquartered company said in a release.

Kardex selected Infosys for its extensive experience in executing large-scale digital transformation projects with SAP S/4HANA and its deep expertise in the manufacturing and logistics sectors. To ensure seamless data transfer, minimise downtime, and maintain data integrity, Infosys is leveraging its own tools, including Infosys Data Services Suite (iDSS), a data management solution.

Infosys aims to create a unified system for Kardex, to equip it with real-time insights, enhanced customer experience, and a strong foundation for growth.

“Our mission is to empower our customers to optimise their intralogistics operations, enhancing efficiency, agility, and overall success," Thomas Reist, Chief Financial Officer of Kardex, was quoted as saying in the release.

Reist said Infosys' expertise in process transformation, supported by SAP solutions, and a proven track record of successful implementations makes it the ideal partner to achieve Kardex's strategic objectives.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Jasmeet Singh, Executive Vice President and Global Head of Manufacturing, Infosys, said the manufacturing and logistics industry is undergoing rapid digital transformation. "We are confident that our data-centric, cloud-first approach will ensure a seamless transition and unlock the full potential of digital transformation," he said.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: No ‘screaming opportunities’ but reasonably valued stocks available in small- and mid-cap space: Groww AMC’s Anupam Tiwari
data.description:
representative image

While valuation matters, long-term investors should not get worried by short-term fluctuations, says Anupam Tiwari, Head of Equity at Groww AMC. The current market correction offers good opportunities in certain areas and while there may not be “screaming opportunities”, there are reasonably valued stocks in small and mid-caps, he says while adding that growth for India will come from all market caps, and a multi-cap approach minimises volatility and helps capture the growth. In a conversation with Moneycontrol, Tiwari spoke about the fund house’s latest multi-cap offering, why he continues to be bullish on India and why concerns on GDP numbers or SIP numbers do not influence his views on the strength of Indian equities.

Edited excerpts:

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

What has been causing the ongoing market volatility?

The market shift began in September when China launched a stimulus, causing a 20-25% spike. Foreign investors, who were underweight on China and overweight on India, reacted by selling. This continued through October and November. Earnings started coming in below expectations, especially outside IT, causing concerns. The market has dropped approximately 9%, with earnings cuts around 8-9%, aligning with the revisions. The US elections and a stronger dollar added further uncertainty, weakening emerging markets, including China and India. We think that this volatility could persist until Q4.

Are valuations a concern?

Valuation matters but long-term investors should not get worried by short-term fluctuations. The current market correction offers good opportunities in certain areas. While there may not be screaming opportunities, there are reasonably valued stocks in small- and mid-cap space.

What does this mean for a fund manager like you?

From our side, we are always aligned with the earnings upgrade and downgrade cycle. Our portfolios have been adjusted accordingly. We remain bullish on certain sectors even after some stock corrections. Specifically, we are still very positive on banking, premium consumption, and a combination of infrastructure and capital goods.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

What other sectors have you adjusted in your portfolio?

We have increased our exposure to telecom. While we have kept our position in banking at the same weight, we are monitoring third-quarter numbers to assess further action. So, for now, we are equal-weight in banking. On the other hand, we are underweight in consumer staples and oil & gas. In terms of discretionary consumption, we are overweight on premium segments and will maintain this position. Our continued underweight on oil and gas is driven by a lack of sustainable improvement in return on equity (ROE). Despite potential short-term trading opportunities, the broader outlook isn’t attractive due to weakening demand and low GRMs, we don’t see a significant improvement in ROEs, which is crucial for stock re-rating.

We are also reassessing our position in pharma. Last year, pharma did well, and we were slightly underweight. We are now closely looking at this sector. We do not think that the Trump administration’s plan to impose tariffs can cause big problems for Indian generic companies in terms of competitive advantage as US dependence on Indian generics is reasonably high which can’t be shifted quickly.

What about IT?

IT is well-positioned. After a tough 2023, the sector is expected to benefit from easing recession fears and increasing IT spending, especially in the US and Europe. Clients’ dependence on Indian IT companies is reasonably high and that is a big strategic advantage for the sector.

Why are you focusing on multi-cap funds?

We are bullish on India’s growth over the next 10-15 years. India has improved in capability, capacity, and confidence. Stronger enterprises, improved infrastructure investments, and technological capacity give India a competitive edge. Growth will come from all market caps, and a multi-cap approach minimizes volatility while capturing growth across sectors.

How do you view FII flows?

Despite potential selling, India’s deep market absorbed more than $12 billion in outflows without significant impact. I am positive about India’s ability to manage this.

What is your take on the slight slowdown in SIPs?

The Indian market’s equity participation is still low, but I expect a generational shift toward more equity investments over time. While monthly net flows may see some fluctuations, the long-term trend remains positive.

What is your outlook for next year? Are there any concerns about GDP growth?

Earnings should recover by Q4. Though Q3 might be weak due to slower government spending, I am positive for the upcoming year. The market will likely discount earnings upgrades soon. The GDP slowdown was expected. We are focusing on long-term trends, not short-term GDP fluctuations. The economy should bounce back.
